ground_truth.title: 6 Steps You Can Take to Achieve Healthy Weight Loss
ground_truth.author: Angela Williams
ground_truth.main_content: When you think about losing weight, extreme diets and lots of cardio may come to mind, but is that really the right strategy for healthy weight loss? The answer is no. You might lose weight if you go on a fad diet or spend hours on the treadmill, but odds are good those pounds will come sneaking back. As Dr. Stacy Ogbeide, a board-certified clinical health psychologist with UT Health San Antonio explains, the key to healthy weight loss—the kind that sticks around for the long-term—is to build healthy habits you can sustain over time. Slow and steady is the recipe for healthy weight loss. According to health experts, losing 1 to 2 pounds each week is a good plan for building new healthy habits and achieving sustainable weight loss. In order to engage in healthy weight loss while maintaining good health, it’s important to build healthy habits that you can live with for a long time. Your Path to Healthy Weight Loss: 1. Be SMART with Your Goals - Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time Bound. 2. Make a Date With Exercise - Schedule workouts like appointments. 3. Fill Your Plate Strategically - Fill half with fruits and vegetables, half with whole grains and lean protein. 4. Choose Fewer Processed Foods - Choose whole foods over heavily processed options. 5. Make Sure You're Eating Enough - Not eating enough can backfire and lead to the restriction-binge cycle. 6. Think Progress, Not Perfection - Set SMART goals and give yourself wiggle room, eating healthy 80% of the time.
with: The key to healthy weight loss—the kind that sticks around for the long-term—is to build healthy habits you can sustain over time.
Slow and steady is the recipe for healthy weight loss. According to health experts, losing 1 to 2 pounds each week is a good plan for building new healthy habits.
When setting health goals, you want to make them SMART: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time Bound.
Not eating enough can actually backfire when it comes to losing weight, leading to the restriction-binge cycle.
Healthy weight loss isn't a pass or fail proposition. Every healthy choice you make is a step in the right direction.
